page: Amazon Web Services i8g.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading, providing a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 22,500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations place the i8g.24xlarge in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Its memory architecture delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for large blocks, alongside top-tier Passmark memory and database operation scores. Furthermore, it demonstrates top-tier throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving, while single-core performance generally registers in the average to strong tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of local NVMe SSD storage combined with the power-efficient ARM64 architecture provides a highly optimized resource profile for data-intensive workloads without the overhead of external storage attachments. However, the lack of x86 instruction set compatibility and the absence of hardware GPU acceleration represent notable tradeoffs for legacy software or heavy deep learning training. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database operation speeds, including Redis SET operations and static web serving. While it lacks GPU acceleration and x86 instruction support, its high-density local storage and memory capacity offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive environments. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.24xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 22.5 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.24xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 96 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 22.5 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform delivering ninety-six dedicated cores, massive local NVMe capacity, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
22,500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
768.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis, and multi-core benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data-intensive workloads
